name: John Cornelius
title: Manufacturing Operations & Continuous Improvement Leader
summary: Manufacturing operations and continuous improvement leader with current multi-site scope, plant-facing management experience, practical analytics fluency, and a 20-year Army aviation foundation. Supports 9 facilities, manages a 40+ project improvement portfolio tied to $4.2M+ in annualized savings, and builds operating routines that help leaders see priorities, remove losses, coach teams, and sustain gains.

Plant Operations: Daily management routines and balanced scorecards
Production priorities, maintenance constraints, material flow, and safety
Continuous Improvement: DMAIC, Lean, TPM, Hoshin Kanri, and standard work
OEE, downtime, scrap, safety, and cost improvement
Rapid Improvement Events and benefits tracking
Analytics & Systems: Power BI, Python, SQL, MES, ERP, and SCADA data
Leadership: Plant manager and functional leader partnership
Army aviation standards and risk management

experience.title: AI & Six Sigma Lead / Operations & Quality Leader
duties: Progressed from quality/materials execution into operations management and multi-site CI/analytics support.
Managed 24/7 continuous-process manufacturing routines, coordinating production priorities, maintenance constraints, material flow, quality, safety, staffing coverage, and shift execution.
Support process optimization, CI governance, and analytics-enabled improvement across 9 manufacturing facilities.
Support Hoshin Kanri policy deployment and balanced-scorecard routines that align plant-level improvement priorities with business objectives.
Champion TPM practices including Autonomous Maintenance, Planned Maintenance, and Focused Improvement to help plants reduce recurring losses and stabilize execution.
achievements: Manage a 40+ project DMAIC and continuous-improvement portfolio tied to $4.2M+ in annualized savings.
Contributed to targeted improvements including 15% scrap reduction, 22% downtime reduction, 35% fewer safety incidents, and 12-18% OEE improvement where measured.
Reduced blowdown oil waste by 70.8% using BrainCube analytics and airflow optimization, delivering $189K in verified annual savings.
Lead and support 30+ Rapid Improvement Events annually, helping teams define problems, test countermeasures, document results, and present business impact.
Train and coach 60+ Green Belts and analysts on structured problem solving, data analysis, practical AI tools, and business-case communication.

experience.title: Army Aviation Operations & Standardization Leader (Retired CW3)
duties: Led aviation operations and readiness programs involving 30-250+ personnel, equipment accountability, logistics, maintenance coordination, and safety-critical execution.
Managed operating requirements involving $10M+ budgets, aircraft readiness, training resources, scheduling constraints, and risk controls.
Built training, evaluation, standards, and risk-management systems that improved readiness, accountability, and disciplined execution.
achievements: Completed a 20-year Army career, retiring as a Chief Warrant Officer 3 with UH-60/MH-60 aviation, instructor pilot, and operations leadership experience.
Designed and executed learning and development programs that progressed 55 pilots to mission-qualified status.
Used post-mission debriefs, root-cause analysis, and standards reviews to improve training throughput, cycle adherence, and operational learning.

education.degree: M.S. - Unmanned Systems, Human Factors Engineering

affiliations: Civil Air Patrol - sUAS training and community programs
Community volunteer leadership in Eastern Panhandle, WV
